title: Client Distribution by Type (Wired/Wireless/Guest)
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client`. from the `client` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per client per poll (can be very high volume). RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `macAddress` (string), `hostType` (string), `connectionType` (string), `healthScore{}` (object), `ssid` (string), `vlanId` (string), `location` (string).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" | stats dc(macAddress) as client_count by connectionType, ssid | sort -client_count | head 20
description: Breaks down the client population by connection type (wired, wireless 2.4GHz, wireless 5GHz, wireless 6GHz) and SSID for capacity planning.
value: Knowing how clients distribute across SSIDs and connection types helps identify overloaded access points and plan wireless capacity.
implementation: Enable the `client` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls client detail data from the Catalyst Center Intent API every 60 minutes. Key fields: `macAddress`, `hostType`, `connectionType`, `ssid`, `vlanId`, `location`, `healthScore`.
visualization: Bar or column chart (client_count by connectionType and ssid), table of top SSIDs, single value for total wireless clients if post-processed.

references.title: Catalyst Center Client Detail API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives: SSID and `connectionType` values can be **null** or **Other** for some wired or onboarding clients, so “top 20” may hide a busy VLAN that does not map cleanly to the fields you chart. A single **captive** or **conference** SSID can **spike** during an **event** without a **chronic** capacity problem, which still looks like a “hot” row in the table.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) ≥1.0 with the **client** or **clienthealth** modular input enabled, writing to `index=catalyst` with `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:client` or `cisco:dnac:clienthealth`.
• Catalyst Center **2.3.5+** with Assurance licensing for full client visibility (wired, wireless, and guest).
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE** to read Assurance client data.
• Wireless controllers and switches managed by Catalyst Center must be in a healthy, onboarded state for client data to be reported.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
The TA polls `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-health` or `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/client-detail` at the configured interval (default 900 seconds). Each event represents one connected client.

Key fields for client type distribution:
• `connectionType`: "WIRED", "WIRELESS", or in some versions "WIRED_GUEST" / "WIRELESS_GUEST". This is the primary field for type classification.
• `macAddress`: unique client MAC address.
• `deviceType`: client device type (laptop, phone, IoT).
• `ssid`: SSID name (wireless clients only; null/empty for wired).
• `siteId`: site UUID where the client is connected.
• `healthScore`: client experience score (0-10 or 0-100 depending on version).
• `hostType`: may indicate "GUEST" for guest clients.

Expected volume: ~1 event per connected client per poll. A 10,000-user campus generates ~10,000 events per poll cycle.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:client" OR sourcetype="cisco:dnac:clienthealth"
| eval client_type=case(connectionType="WIRELESS" AND (hostType="GUEST" OR ssid="*Guest*"), "Guest", connectionType="WIRELESS", "Wireless", connectionType="WIRED", "Wired", 1==1, "Unknown")
| stats dc(macAddress) as client_count by client_type, siteId
| eventstats sum(client_count) as total_clients by siteId
| eval pct=round(client_count/total_clients*100,1)
| sort siteId, -client_count
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`eval client_type=case(...)`**: Classifies clients into Wired, Wireless, or Guest based on `connectionType` and guest detection heuristics. Guest detection varies by deployment — some use `hostType="GUEST"`, others rely on SSID naming conventions (e.g., "*Guest*").
• **`dc(macAddress) as client_count`**: Counts distinct clients per type. Using `dc(macAddress)` prevents double-counting clients that appear in multiple poll events.
• **`eventstats sum(client_count) as total_clients by siteId`**: Computes the total client count per site for percentage calculation.
• **`eval pct`**: Shows each client type as a percentage of the site total — useful for identifying sites with unusual client type distributions.

Step 3 — Validate
• **UI comparison:** Open Catalyst Center > Assurance > Health > Client Health. The summary tiles show wired, wireless, and total client counts. Compare with the Splunk `dc(macAddress)` totals for the same time window. Allow ±5% variance due to poll timing.
• **Guest detection:** Verify guest client classification by checking known guest SSID names against the Splunk results.
• **Per-site check:** Select a specific site in Catalyst Center and compare the client type breakdown with the Splunk per-site results.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Pie chart showing client type distribution (Wired/Wireless/Guest), stacked bar chart by site, and a timechart showing client count trends by type over 7 days.
• **Capacity planning:** Use the distribution data to identify sites with high wireless-to-wired ratios (may need AP capacity expansion) or sites with unexpectedly high guest usage (may need guest SSID capacity review).
• **Trend analysis:** Compare weekly client count trends to identify growth patterns and capacity planning needs.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **All clients show as "Unknown":** The `connectionType` field may not be populated or may use different values than expected. Run `| stats count by connectionType` to identify the actual values.
• **Guest clients not detected:** Guest classification depends on your deployment's guest identification method. Update the case statement to match your SSID naming or hostType convention.
• **Client count much lower than expected:** The TA may be hitting API pagination limits. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for pagination errors.
• **Duplicate clients across sites:** Clients roaming between sites may appear in multiple sites within a single poll window. Use `| dedup macAddress sortby -_time` to take the latest site assignment.
• **No client events arriving:** verify the `client` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **NULL or zero values in health scores:** some per-client fields (RSSI, SNR) are only populated for wireless clients; empty values for wired clients are expected.
• **More events than expected:** the client API generates one event per connected client per poll; if credential access is denied, check the service account role.
grandmaExplanation: We show you how many devices are on each wireless network and whether they are using wired or wireless connections.
